tables.title: The H's and T's of Reversible Causes
rows: Hemorrhage, dehydration, poor skin turgor, flat neck veins
Oxygen, ensure airway patency, advanced airway
Ventilation optimization, sodium bicarbonate if indicated
Known renal failure, ECG changes (peaked T waves or flat T/U waves)
Absent breath sounds, tracheal deviation, JVD, hypotension
JVD, muffled heart sounds, pulsus paradoxus, hypotension
Drug ingestion history, wide QRS (TCA), pill bottles at scene
Specific antidotes (NaHCO₃ for TCA, naloxone for opioids)
Sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, right heart strain, DVT risk factors
Chest pain, ST changes, known CAD, ACS history

tables.title: Asystole/PEA vs VF/pVT: Key Differences
rows: As soon as feasible, ideally within 3–5 min
Amiodarone or lidocaine for refractory cases
May be considered if manual impractical
Same — not routine but considered in specific circumstances
